tluaMetatableClassIdiom.tlua(35,3): error TS2739: Type '{ balance: number; }' is missing the following properties from type 'Account': deposit, withdraw


==== tluaMetatableClassIdiom.tlua (1 errors) ====
    // The Lua class idiom: a method table behind __index, and a constructor that pairs a fresh
    // table with it. The constructor's result satisfies the interface only because of the
    // fallback -- the table it builds has a balance and nothing else.
    
    interface Account {
      balance: number;
      deposit: (self: Account, amount: number) => void;
      withdraw: (self: Account, amount: number) => boolean;
    }
    
    local AccountClass = {
      deposit = function(self: Account, amount: number) self.balance = self.balance + amount; end,
      withdraw = function(self: Account, amount: number): boolean
        if self.balance < amount then
          return false;
        end
        self.balance = self.balance - amount;
        return true;
      end,
    };
    
    local AccountMeta = { __index = AccountClass };
    
    function newAccount(balance: number): Account
      return setmetatable({ balance = balance }, AccountMeta);
    end
    
    local account = newAccount(100);
    account.deposit(account, 50);
    local ok: boolean = account.withdraw(account, 20);
    local balance: number = account.balance;
    
    // Without the pairing, the same table does not.
    function missingMethods(balance: number): Account
      return { balance = balance };
      ~~~~~~
!!! error TS2739: Type '{ balance: number; }' is missing the following properties from type 'Account': deposit, withdraw
    end
    
    // A table paired with the method table is still a table: pairs iterates it.
    local paired = setmetatable({ balance = 1 }, AccountMeta);
    for k, v in pairs(paired) do
      local key: string = k;
    end
